reading.title: Text 1: My Daily Routine
reading.content:
My name is Lucas. I am 25 years old and I am from Belo Horizonte, Brazil. I live in a small apartment near the center of the city with my cat, Mimi. I work as a waiter in a big restaurant.

Every day, I wake up at six o'clock in the morning. First, I take a shower and get dressed. Then, I have breakfast in the kitchen. I usually eat bread with butter and drink a cup of coffee. Sometimes, I also eat a banana or an apple.

I leave my apartment at seven thirty. I go to work by bus. The bus stop is near my apartment, so I walk for about five minutes. The bus ride takes about thirty minutes. I arrive at the restaurant at eight fifteen.

I work from eight thirty in the morning to five o'clock in the afternoon. The restaurant is very busy at lunchtime, from twelve to two o'clock. Many people come to eat every day. I serve food and drinks to the customers. I also clean the tables and help in the kitchen sometimes. My colleagues are very friendly, and I like working with them.

After work, I usually go to the gym. The gym is close to the restaurant, so I walk there. I exercise for about one hour. I like running on the treadmill and lifting weights. Exercise makes me feel good and helps me relax after a long day.

I get home at about seven o'clock in the evening. I take another shower and then I cook dinner. I like cooking. My favorite food is pasta with tomato sauce and chicken. After dinner, I wash the dishes and sit on the sofa in the living room. I watch TV or read a book. Sometimes, I talk to my family on the phone. My parents and my sister live in Belo Horizonte, but in a different neighborhood.

I usually go to bed at ten thirty. Mimi always sleeps on my bed with me. On weekends, I wake up later, at about nine o'clock. On Saturdays, I go shopping at the supermarket and clean my apartment. In the afternoon, I meet my friends and we go to the park, to the cinema, or to a café. On Sundays, I visit my parents and we have lunch together. My mother cooks very well. I love her food.

I am happy with my life. My job is tiring, but I have good friends, a loving family, and a cute cat. What more do I need?

### Glossary

| Word / Expression | Tradução |
|-------------------|----------|
| daily routine | rotina diária |
| waiter | garçom |
| get dressed | se vestir |
| bus ride | viagem de ônibus |
| busy | movimentado / ocupado |
| customers | clientes |
| colleagues | colegas de trabalho |
| treadmill | esteira |
| lifting weights | levantar pesos |
| neighborhood | bairro |
| tiring | cansativo |

### Comprehension Questions

**1. Where does Lucas live, and who does he live with?**

**2. How does Lucas go to work, and how long does it take?**

**3. What does Lucas do at the restaurant?**

**4. What does Lucas do after work on weekdays?**

**5. What does Lucas do on Sundays?**

### Answer Key — Text 1

1. Lucas lives in a small apartment near the center of Belo Horizonte, Brazil. He lives with his cat, Mimi.
2. Lucas goes to work by bus. The bus ride takes about thirty minutes.
3. Lucas serves food and drinks to customers, cleans the tables, and sometimes helps in the kitchen.
4. After work on weekdays, Lucas usually goes to the gym, exercises for about one hour, then goes home, takes a shower, cooks dinner, washes the dishes, and watches TV or reads a book.
5. On Sundays, Lucas visits his parents and they have lunch together.

---

reading.title: Text 2: A New Student
reading.content:
My name is Sofia and I am eighteen years old. I am from São Paulo, Brazil, but now I am living in London, England. I am a student at a language school in the center of the city. I am studying English because I want to work in an international company in the future.

I arrived in London three weeks ago. At first, everything was very difficult. I did not understand people when they spoke to me because they spoke very fast. The weather was also very different. In São Paulo, it is usually warm and sunny, but in London, it is often cold and rainy. I was surprised because it rained almost every day in my first week.

I live in a house with four other international students. Their names are Yuki, Marco, Fatima, and Hans. Yuki is from Japan, Marco is from Italy, Fatima is from Morocco, and Hans is from Germany. We all speak different languages, so we communicate in English. This is very good practice for all of us.

Our house has a big kitchen, a living room, two bathrooms, and five bedrooms. My bedroom is small, but it is comfortable. There is a bed, a desk, a wardrobe, and a window with a view of the garden. I like my room.

Every day, I go to school by subway. The school is in a building near Oxford Street, which is a famous street for shopping. My classes start at nine o'clock and finish at one o'clock in the afternoon. We study grammar, vocabulary, reading, writing, listening, and speaking. My teacher is called Mr. Williams. He is very patient and kind. There are twelve students in my class. They are from different countries: Spain, South Korea, Turkey, Colombia, and Egypt.

After class, I usually have lunch with my friends. We like to try different restaurants. London has food from all over the world: Indian, Chinese, Japanese, Mexican, and many more. My favorite so far is a small Italian restaurant near the school. The pasta there is delicious.

In the afternoon, I study at the library or at home. I do my homework, practice my pronunciation, and learn new vocabulary. Sometimes, I watch British TV shows to improve my listening. I think that watching shows in English is very helpful.

On weekends, I explore London. There are so many things to see and do. I already visited the British Museum, Buckingham Palace, and the Tower of London. I took many photos. I also walked in Hyde Park and went to Camden Market. Everything is very interesting.

I miss my family and friends in Brazil sometimes. I call my parents every weekend on video. My mother always asks if I am eating well and if I am warm enough. I tell her that I am fine and that I am learning a lot.

I am going to stay in London for six months. After that, I want to take an English exam and then go back to Brazil to look for a job. I think this experience is going to help me a lot in my career. I am nervous sometimes, but I am also very excited. Learning a new language is not easy, but it is an amazing adventure.

### Glossary

| Word / Expression | Tradução |
|-------------------|----------|
| language school | escola de idiomas |
| arrived | cheguei / chegou |
| at first | no começo |
| surprised | surpresa |
| communicate | comunicar-se |
| practice | prática |
| comfortable | confortável |
| view | vista |
| patient | paciente |
| pronunciation | pronúncia |
| improve | melhorar |
| explore | explorar |
| already | já |
| miss | sentir falta |
| career | carreira |
| adventure | aventura |

### Comprehension Questions

**1. Why is Sofia studying English in London?**

**2. Where does Sofia live, and who are her housemates?**

**3. What does Sofia do after class in the afternoon?**

**4. What places did Sofia already visit in London?**

**5. How long is Sofia going to stay in London, and what are her plans after that?**

### Answer Key — Text 2

1. Sofia is studying English in London because she wants to work in an international company in the future.
2. Sofia lives in a house with four other international students: Yuki from Japan, Marco from Italy, Fatima from Morocco, and Hans from Germany.
3. After class, Sofia studies at the library or at home, does her homework, practices her pronunciation, learns new vocabulary, and sometimes watches British TV shows.
4. Sofia already visited the British Museum, Buckingham Palace, the Tower of London, Hyde Park, and Camden Market.
5. Sofia is going to stay in London for six months. After that, she wants to take an English exam and go back to Brazil to look for a job.

---

reading.title: Text 3: The Silva Family
reading.content:
The Silva family lives in a house in Curitiba, in the south of Brazil. The family has five members: Carlos, Rita, Pedro, Ana, and Grandma Lúcia.

Carlos is the father. He is forty-five years old. He is a tall man with short brown hair and brown eyes. He works as an engineer at a construction company. He goes to work by car every day. He leaves the house at seven in the morning and comes back at six in the evening. Carlos likes his job, but he says it is very stressful sometimes. On weekends, he likes to relax. He watches soccer on TV, reads the newspaper, and sometimes goes fishing with his friends.

Rita is the mother. She is forty-two years old. She has long black hair and dark brown eyes. She is a nurse at a big hospital in the city. She works from seven in the morning to three in the afternoon. She loves her job because she likes helping people. Rita is also a very good cook. She makes delicious food every day. Her specialty is a traditional Brazilian bean stew called feijoada. The whole family loves Rita's feijoada.

Pedro is the son. He is seventeen years old. He is tall, like his father, and he has short black hair. Pedro is a student at a public high school. He is in the last year and he is studying hard because he wants to go to university next year. He wants to study computer science. Pedro is very good with computers and technology. He can fix computers and create websites. In his free time, Pedro likes playing video games with his friends online and listening to music. He also plays basketball at school on Wednesdays and Fridays.

Ana is the daughter. She is twelve years old. She has long brown hair and big brown eyes. She is in the seventh grade at the same school as her brother. Ana is very creative and artistic. She likes drawing, painting, and making crafts. Her bedroom walls are full of her drawings. She is also very good at English. She watches English-language cartoons and movies, and she practices with a language app on her phone every day. Ana wants to be a fashion designer when she grows up.

Grandma Lúcia is Rita's mother. She is seventy years old. She moved to the Silva family's house two years ago, after Grandpa João died. Grandma Lúcia has white hair and wears glasses. She is a very kind and funny woman. She tells amazing stories about her childhood in a small town in Minas Gerais. Grandma Lúcia likes gardening. There are many beautiful flowers and plants in the garden because of her. She also knits sweaters and scarves for the family in winter.

The Silva family is very close. They always have dinner together at seven thirty in the evening. At the table, they talk about their day. On Saturday mornings, they clean the house together. Everyone has a task: Carlos vacuums the floors, Rita does the laundry, Pedro washes the dishes, Ana cleans the bathrooms, and Grandma Lúcia waters the plants.

On Sunday mornings, Carlos and Pedro go to the bakery and buy fresh bread, cheese, and ham for breakfast. The whole family eats together in the dining room. After breakfast, they sometimes go to the park or visit relatives. On special occasions, like birthdays, they have a big party with barbecue, music, and cake. The Silvas love celebrating together.

Life is not always easy. Carlos and Rita work very hard, Pedro is stressed about his exams, and Ana sometimes misses her grandfather. But they support each other and they are always there when someone needs help. The Silva family believes that love and unity are the most important things in life.

### Glossary

| Word / Expression | Tradução |
|-------------------|----------|
| construction company | empresa de construção |
| stressful | estressante |
| fishing | pescar |
| bean stew | feijoada (ensopado de feijão) |
| specialty | especialidade |
| high school | ensino médio |
| computer science | ciência da computação |
| fix | consertar |
| crafts | artesanato |
| fashion designer | designer de moda |
| grow up | crescer |
| moved | mudou-se |
| died | morreu / faleceu |
| gardening | jardinagem |
| knits | tricota |
| vacuums | aspira (com aspirador) |
| bakery | padaria |
| barbecue | churrasco |
| support | apoiar |
| unity | união |

### Comprehension Questions

**1. How many people are in the Silva family, and what are their names?**

**2. What are the parents' jobs, and what time do they work?**

**3. What does Pedro want to study at university, and why?**

**4. What are Ana's hobbies, and what does she want to be when she grows up?**

**5. What does the Silva family do together on Saturdays and Sundays?**

### Answer Key — Text 3

1. There are five people in the Silva family: Carlos (the father), Rita (the mother), Pedro (the son), Ana (the daughter), and Grandma Lúcia (Rita's mother).
2. Carlos is an engineer at a construction company. He works from seven in the morning to six in the evening. Rita is a nurse at a big hospital. She works from seven in the morning to three in the afternoon.
3. Pedro wants to study computer science because he is very good with computers and technology. He can fix computers and create websites.
4. Ana's hobbies are drawing, painting, and making crafts. She also likes watching English-language cartoons and movies, and practicing with a language app. She wants to be a fashion designer when she grows up.
5. On Saturdays, the Silva family cleans the house together. On Sundays, Carlos and Pedro go to the bakery to buy fresh bread for breakfast. After breakfast, they sometimes go to the park or visit relatives.

---

communication.title: Dialogue 1: At the Hotel — Checking In
communication.content:
**Setting:** A tourist arrives at a hotel in London and speaks to the receptionist at the front desk.

---

**Receptionist:** Good evening, sir. Welcome to The Grand Central Hotel. How can I help you?

**Guest:** Good evening. I have a reservation. My name is Roberto Almeida.

**Receptionist:** Let me check... Almeida. Can you spell your last name for me, please?

**Guest:** Of course. A-L-M-E-I-D-A.

**Receptionist:** Thank you. Yes, I can see your reservation. A single room for three nights, from March 20th to March 23rd. Is that correct?

**Guest:** Yes, that's right.

**Receptionist:** Perfect. Can I see your passport, please?

**Guest:** Sure, here you are.

**Receptionist:** Thank you. And how would you like to pay — by credit card or cash?

**Guest:** By credit card, please.

**Receptionist:** Alright. Can you insert your card here, please?... Great, the payment is confirmed. Here is your room key. Your room number is 405. It is on the fourth floor. The elevator is on your right.

**Guest:** Thank you. What time is breakfast?

**Receptionist:** Breakfast is from seven to ten in the morning. It is served in the restaurant on the ground floor.

**Guest:** Is there Wi-Fi in the room?

**Receptionist:** Yes, there is free Wi-Fi in all rooms. The network name is "GrandCentral" and the password is on this card.

**Guest:** Great, thank you. One more question — is there a parking lot?

**Receptionist:** Yes, we have underground parking. It costs fifteen pounds per night. Would you like to use it?

**Guest:** No, thank you. I don't have a car. I arrived by train.

**Receptionist:** No problem. Is there anything else I can help you with?

**Guest:** Actually, yes. Can you recommend a good restaurant near the hotel for dinner?

**Receptionist:** Of course. There is an Italian restaurant called "Bella Vita" on the next street. The food is very good and it is not very expensive.

**Guest:** That sounds great. How far is it?

**Receptionist:** It is about a five-minute walk. Turn left when you leave the hotel, walk to the end of the street, and it is on the right.

**Guest:** Perfect. Thank you very much.

**Receptionist:** You're welcome, Mr. Almeida. Enjoy your stay!

**Guest:** Thank you. Good night.

**Receptionist:** Good night, sir.

---

communication.title: Dialogue 2: At the Restaurant — Ordering a Meal
communication.content:
**Setting:** Two friends, Carolina and Marcos, are at a restaurant for lunch.

---

**Waiter:** Good afternoon. Welcome to The Green Olive. A table for two?

**Carolina:** Good afternoon. Yes, please.

**Waiter:** This way, please. Here is your table. Would you like to sit here, near the window?

**Marcos:** Yes, this is nice. Thank you.

**Waiter:** Here are the menus. I'll give you a few minutes to decide. Would you like something to drink while you wait?

**Carolina:** Yes, can I have a glass of orange juice, please?

**Marcos:** And I'll have water, please. Still water, not sparkling.

**Waiter:** One orange juice and one still water. I'll be right back.

*(A few minutes later)*

**Waiter:** Here are your drinks. Are you ready to order?

**Carolina:** I think so. I'd like the chicken salad, please. Is it a big portion?

**Waiter:** It is a medium portion, but it comes with bread on the side.

**Carolina:** That's fine. I'll have that.

**Waiter:** And for you, sir?

**Marcos:** I'd like the fish and chips, please. Is the fish fresh?

**Waiter:** Yes, it is. We get fresh fish every morning.

**Marcos:** Great. I'll have the fish and chips then.

**Waiter:** Would you like any starters? We have a very good tomato soup today.

**Carolina:** No starters for me, thank you.

**Marcos:** Me neither. But maybe a dessert later.

**Waiter:** Of course. I'll bring your food soon.

*(Fifteen minutes later)*

**Waiter:** Here you go. The chicken salad for you, madam, and the fish and chips for you, sir. Enjoy your meal.

**Carolina:** Thank you. This looks delicious.

**Marcos:** Thank you.

*(After eating)*

**Waiter:** How was everything? Did you enjoy your meal?

**Carolina:** It was very good, thank you.

**Marcos:** The fish was excellent. Can we see the dessert menu, please?

**Waiter:** Of course. Here it is.

**Marcos:** I'll have the chocolate cake, please.

**Carolina:** And I'd like a cup of coffee, please. Black, no sugar.

**Waiter:** One chocolate cake and one black coffee. Coming right up.

*(After dessert)*

**Marcos:** Can we have the bill, please?

**Waiter:** Certainly. Here you are. The total is thirty-two pounds and fifty pence.

**Marcos:** Can we pay by card?

**Waiter:** Yes, of course. Here is the card machine.

**Carolina:** Let's split the bill. I'll pay my half.

**Marcos:** OK. That's sixteen pounds and twenty-five pence each.

**Waiter:** Thank you. Here are your receipts. Have a lovely afternoon.

**Carolina:** Thank you. The food was really good. We'll come back.

**Waiter:** That's great to hear. You're welcome anytime. Goodbye!

**Marcos:** Bye!

---

communication.title: Dialogue 3: At the Doctor's Office
communication.content:
**Setting:** A patient, Fernanda, goes to see a doctor because she is feeling unwell.

---

**Receptionist:** Good morning. Do you have an appointment?

**Fernanda:** Good morning. Yes, I have an appointment at ten o'clock. My name is Fernanda Costa.

**Receptionist:** Let me check... Yes, here it is. Dr. Thompson is your doctor today. Please take a seat in the waiting room. The doctor will see you in a few minutes.

**Fernanda:** Thank you.

*(Ten minutes later)*

**Doctor:** Fernanda Costa?

**Fernanda:** Yes, that's me.

**Doctor:** Hello, Fernanda. I'm Dr. Thompson. Please come in and sit down. How are you today?

**Fernanda:** Hello, doctor. Not very well, I'm afraid.

**Doctor:** I'm sorry to hear that. What's the problem? What are your symptoms?

**Fernanda:** Well, I have a very bad headache. I also have a sore throat and I am very tired. My body hurts all over.

**Doctor:** I see. How long have you had these symptoms?

**Fernanda:** Since yesterday morning. I also felt very cold last night. I think I had a fever.

**Doctor:** OK. Let me take your temperature... It is thirty-eight point five degrees. Yes, you do have a slight fever. Let me check your throat... Open your mouth and say "ah."

**Fernanda:** Ah...

**Doctor:** Your throat is red and swollen. Do you have a cough?

**Fernanda:** Yes, a little. And my nose is running.

**Doctor:** Do you have any allergies?

**Fernanda:** No, I don't.

**Doctor:** Are you taking any medication at the moment?

**Fernanda:** No, I'm not.

**Doctor:** OK. I think you have the flu. It is very common at this time of year. I'm going to prescribe some medication for you. Take these pills twice a day, in the morning and in the evening, after eating. You also need to drink a lot of water and rest.

**Fernanda:** How long do I need to rest?

**Doctor:** I recommend at least three days of rest. Don't go to work until you feel better. Stay at home, keep warm, and sleep as much as you can.

**Fernanda:** Should I come back if I don't feel better?

**Doctor:** Yes. If you still have a fever after three days, or if your symptoms get worse, please come back or call the clinic.

**Fernanda:** OK, doctor. Thank you very much.

**Doctor:** You're welcome. Take care of yourself. I hope you feel better soon.

**Fernanda:** Thank you. Goodbye.

**Doctor:** Goodbye, Fernanda.

*(Fernanda goes back to the reception)*

**Fernanda:** Excuse me. Where is the nearest pharmacy?

**Receptionist:** There is one on King Street, about two minutes from here. Turn right when you leave the building.

**Fernanda:** Thank you very much.

**Receptionist:** You're welcome. Get well soon!

---

communication.title: Dialogue 4: Shopping for Clothes
communication.content:
**Setting:** A woman, Juliana, is shopping at a clothing store.

---

**Shop Assistant:** Good morning! Can I help you?

**Juliana:** Good morning. Yes, I'm looking for a dress for a party.

**Shop Assistant:** Of course. What color are you thinking of?

**Juliana:** I'd like something in blue or black.

**Shop Assistant:** We have some beautiful dresses in this section. What size are you?

**Juliana:** I think I am a medium. But I'm not sure about sizes here. I am from Brazil.

**Shop Assistant:** No problem. In our store, medium is size ten. Let me show you some options. How about this blue one? It is very popular.

**Juliana:** Oh, it's lovely! Can I try it on?

**Shop Assistant:** Of course. The fitting rooms are over there, on the left.

*(Juliana tries on the dress)*

**Juliana:** Hmm, I think it is a bit too big. Do you have it in a smaller size?

**Shop Assistant:** Let me check... Yes, here it is in size eight. Try this one.

*(Juliana tries it on again)*

**Juliana:** This is much better. It fits perfectly! How much is it?

**Shop Assistant:** It is forty-five pounds.

**Juliana:** That's a nice price. I'll take it. I also need some shoes. Do you have shoes here?

**Shop Assistant:** Yes, the shoe section is at the back of the store. What kind of shoes are you looking for?

**Juliana:** Some black shoes, comfortable but elegant. For the same party.

**Shop Assistant:** How about these? They have a small heel and they are very comfortable.

**Juliana:** They look nice. Can I try them on? Size thirty-seven, please. I mean, what is that in UK sizes?

**Shop Assistant:** Size thirty-seven is a UK four. Here you are.

**Juliana:** They feel great! I can walk easily in them. How much are they?

**Shop Assistant:** They are thirty pounds.

**Juliana:** Perfect. I'll take the dress and the shoes, please.

**Shop Assistant:** Wonderful. That's seventy-five pounds in total. Cash or card?

**Juliana:** Card, please.

**Shop Assistant:** Here you go. Would you like a bag for them?

**Juliana:** Yes, please.

**Shop Assistant:** Here is your bag and your receipt. You can return or exchange items within thirty days with the receipt.

**Juliana:** Good to know. Thank you for your help!

**Shop Assistant:** My pleasure. I hope you have a wonderful time at the party!

**Juliana:** Thank you! Goodbye.

**Shop Assistant:** Bye! Have a great day.

---

communication.title: Dialogue 5: Asking for Directions
communication.content:
**Setting:** A tourist, Ricardo, is lost in the city center and asks a local for directions.

---

**Ricardo:** Excuse me, I'm sorry to bother you. Can you help me?

**Local:** Of course! What do you need?

**Ricardo:** I'm looking for the Central Library. Do you know where it is?

**Local:** Yes, I do. It is not very far from here. About ten minutes on foot.

**Ricardo:** Oh, that's good. How do I get there?

**Local:** OK, so go straight on this street. Walk for about three minutes. Then, you will see a big church on your left. That is St. Peter's Church.

**Ricardo:** A big church on the left. OK.

**Local:** Yes. At the church, turn right. Walk along that street for about five minutes. You will pass a park on your right side and a pharmacy on your left side.

**Ricardo:** Park on the right, pharmacy on the left. Got it.

**Local:** After the pharmacy, you will come to a crossroads. The library is on the corner, on the left side. It is a large white building. You can't miss it.

**Ricardo:** A large white building on the corner. That sounds easy enough. Thank you so much!

**Local:** You're welcome. Are you a tourist?

**Ricardo:** Yes, I am. I'm from Brazil. I arrived yesterday.

**Local:** Welcome to our city! I hope you enjoy your visit. The library is beautiful inside. There is also a nice café on the ground floor.

**Ricardo:** That's great. I love cafés. One more thing — is there a bus stop near the library? I need to go to the train station later.

**Local:** Yes. There is a bus stop right in front of the library. You can take bus number seven. It goes directly to the train station.

**Ricardo:** That's very helpful. Thank you again for everything!

**Local:** No problem at all. Have a wonderful day!

**Ricardo:** You too! Goodbye.

**Local:** Bye!

---

exercises.title: Exercício 11: Coloque o verbo no Simple Present (terceira pessoa)
exercises.content:
Exemplo: I play → She **plays**

2. We go to school. → She _______ to school.
3. They study English. → He _______ English.
4. I have breakfast at 7. → She _______ breakfast at 7.
5. We do the dishes. → He _______ the dishes.
6. They carry heavy bags. → She _______ heavy bags.
7. I wash my car. → He _______ his car.
8. We try hard. → She _______ hard.
9. I play guitar. → He _______ guitar.
10. They fix computers. → She _______ computers.

exercises.title: Exercício 15: WH-Questions — Complete com a palavra interrogativa correta
exercises.content:
1. _______ is your name? — My name is Ana.
2. _______ do you live? — I live in São Paulo.
3. _______ old are you? — I am 30.
4. _______ do you go to work? — By bus.
5. _______ is your birthday? — In September.
6. _______ do you study English? — Because I need it for work.
7. _______ brothers do you have? — Two.
8. _______ is your teacher? — Mrs. Johnson.
9. _______ does this word mean? — It means "beautiful."
10. _______ do you do? — I am a nurse.

exercises.title: Exercício 19: Escolha a alternativa correta
exercises.content:
1. She _______ to school every day.
   a) go  b) goes  c) going

2. I _______ watching TV right now.
   a) am  b) is  c) are

3. _______ you have any brothers?
   a) Are  b) Does  c) Do

4. He _______ born in 1999.
   a) is  b) was  c) were

5. There _______ some milk in the fridge.
   a) is  b) are  c) have

6. She _______ speak Japanese.
   a) cans  b) can  c) can to

7. I _______ to the gym yesterday.
   a) go  b) went  c) goed

8. My keys are _______ the table.
   a) in  b) at  c) on

9. He doesn't _______ a car.
   a) has  b) have  c) having

10. _______ is your favorite color?
    a) Who  b) What  c) Where

exercises.title: Exercício 20: Escreva frases usando as palavras indicadas (no tempo verbal indicado)
exercises.content:
1. (Simple Present — I / play / guitar / on weekends)
2. (Present Continuous — She / read / a book / now)
3. (Simple Past — We / go / to the beach / last Sunday)
4. (Going to — They / move / to a new house / next month)
5. (Simple Present Negative — He / not like / cold weather)
6. (Simple Present Question — you / have / any pets / ?)
7. (Can — I / not / speak / German)
8. (There is/are — two parks / near my house)
9. (Simple Past Negative — She / not see / the movie)
10. (Imperative — close / the door / please)
